questions.q: Krishnadevaraya was the most celebrated emperor of which empire?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya was the greatest emperor of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.q: In which year did Krishnadevaraya's reign begin?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya reigned from 1509 to 1529 CE.

questions.q: In which year did Krishnadevaraya's reign end?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya ruled until his death in 1529 CE.

questions.q: From which capital city did Krishnadevaraya rule his empire?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya ruled from the Vijayanagara capital at Hampi.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya was a famous patron of literature in which language, among others?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya was a renowned patron of Telugu and Sanskrit literature.

questions.q: Besides patronising poets, Krishnadevaraya was also accomplished as a what?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya was himself an accomplished poet and scholar.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya is generally regarded as leading Vijayanagara to its what?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya led Vijayanagara to its political and cultural zenith.

questions.q: In which century did Krishnadevaraya reign?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya reigned from 1509 to 1529, in the early 16th century CE.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya's empire was a great stronghold of which culture?
questions.explanation: Under Krishnadevaraya, Vijayanagara was a flourishing centre of Hindu culture in the south.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya was famous as a builder and renovator of what?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya built and embellished many temples and towering gopurams across his empire.

questions.q: To which Vijayanagara dynasty did Krishnadevaraya belong?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya belonged to the Tuluva dynasty of Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Which Telugu epic poem on devotion and statecraft was written by Krishnadevaraya himself?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya composed the Telugu work Amuktamalyada, a poem on the saint Andal that also reflects on kingship.

questions.q: The group of eight celebrated poets at Krishnadevaraya's Telugu court was known as the:
questions.explanation: The eight great poets of Krishnadevaraya's court were known as the Ashtadiggajas (eight elephants of the directions).

questions.q: Which leading Telugu poet, author of Manucharitramu, was the foremost of Krishnadevaraya's Ashtadiggajas?
questions.explanation: Allasani Peddana, author of Manucharitramu, was honoured as the greatest of the Ashtadiggajas and called the 'grandfather of Telugu poetry'.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya fought repeated wars against which northern neighbours over the Raichur doab?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya warred with the Deccan sultanates, especially Bijapur, over the contested Raichur doab.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya won a famous 1520 battle that secured the Raichur doab against Bijapur. This is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Raichur in 1520 was a major victory for Krishnadevaraya over the sultanate of Bijapur.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya maintained friendly relations and trade with which European power on the west coast?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya kept cordial trade relations with the Portuguese, who had established themselves at Goa, importing horses and arms.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya led successful eastern campaigns against which rival dynasty of Odisha (Kalinga)?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya campaigned victoriously against the Gajapati rulers of Odisha, extending Vijayanagara's eastern frontier.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya built a major temple at Hampi to commemorate a victory; it is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya built the Krishna temple at Hampi to mark his conquest of Udayagiri in the east.

questions.q: Which Portuguese traveller's account vividly praises Krishnadevaraya as a just and able ruler?
questions.explanation: The Portuguese chronicler Domingo Paes visited during Krishnadevaraya's reign and praised the king and his capital.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya is popularly remembered in folklore for his clever court jester and adviser named:
questions.explanation: Tenali Rama, a witty poet and adviser, is the famous figure associated with Krishnadevaraya's court in popular folklore.

questions.q: The famous statue group at Tirupati's Venkateswara temple includes a figure of Krishnadevaraya alongside whom?
questions.explanation: Bronze and stone images of Krishnadevaraya with his two queens, Tirumala Devi and Chinnadevi, stand at the Tirupati temple he patronised.

questions.q: Whom did Krishnadevaraya succeed on the Vijayanagara throne in 1509?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya succeeded his half-brother Vira Narasimha Raya, the founder of the Tuluva dynasty's rule.

questions.q: Which capable minister served as Krishnadevaraya's chief adviser and prime minister?
questions.explanation: Timmarusu, also called Saluva Timma, was Krishnadevaraya's trusted prime minister and mentor.

questions.q: The Sanskrit title 'Andhra Bhoja' was applied to Krishnadevaraya in recognition of what?
options: His great patronage of learning and letters
questions.explanation: He was hailed as 'Andhra Bhoja', likening his literary patronage to the legendary scholar-king Bhoja.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya's eastern campaign against the Gajapatis was sealed by a marriage alliance with whose daughter?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya concluded peace with the Gajapati ruler Prataparudra by marrying his daughter, sealing the eastern frontier.

questions.q: After capturing the strong fortress of Raichur, Krishnadevaraya's main rival sultan whom he defeated was:
questions.explanation: At the Battle of Raichur (1520), Krishnadevaraya defeated Ismail Adil Shah, the sultan of Bijapur.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya erected one of the tallest gopurams at which great temple town as an act of patronage?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya was a generous patron of temples such as Tirupati, where he funded gateways, gifts, and grand offerings.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya's reign is sometimes called the golden age of which language's classical literature?
questions.explanation: His reign is regarded as a golden age of Telugu literature, especially the flourishing of the Prabandha poetic form.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya's death in 1529 was followed within a few decades by which catastrophe for the empire?
options: The defeat at Talikota and sack of Hampi (1565)
questions.explanation: After Krishnadevaraya's death the empire weakened and was shattered at the Battle of Talikota in 1565, leading to Hampi's destruction.
